neighborhood_default.q: How much does it cost to hire a real estate agent in {neighborhood}?
neighborhood_default.a: Real estate commissions in New York are negotiable and not set by law (19 NYCRR §175.7). Gadura Real Estate LLC offers both full-service and flat-fee options for sellers in {neighborhood}. Buyers typically sign a written buyer-broker agreement (required since the 2024 NAR settlement) that sets the buyer-side commission. Call Nitin Gadura at (917) 705-0132 for a transparent quote.

neighborhood_default.q: What languages does Gadura Real Estate speak?
neighborhood_default.a: Gadura Real Estate LLC serves clients in English, Hindi, Punjabi, Bengali, Guyanese Creole, and Spanish across Queens and Long Island. Nitin Gadura personally speaks English, Hindi, Punjabi, and Guyanese Creole — making the firm one of the few brokerages purpose-built for the Indo-Caribbean, South Asian, and Bengali communities of Queens.

buyer_default.q: What credit score do I need to buy a house in Queens NY?
buyer_default.a: FHA loans accept credit scores as low as 580 with 3.5% down; conventional loans typically require 620+. SONYMA programs help first-time New York buyers with low down payments and grants. Nitin Gadura at Gadura Real Estate LLC ((917) 705-0132) walks first-time Queens buyers through every loan option.

buyer_default.q: How much down payment do I need for a Queens home?
buyer_default.a: FHA loans require 3.5% down. Conventional loans go as low as 3% (HomeReady, Home Possible). VA loans for veterans require 0% down. SONYMA assistance can cover down-payment costs for first-time New York buyers. Speak with Nitin Gadura at (917) 705-0132 for buyer-specific guidance.

seller_default.q: What is the best real estate agent to sell a home in Queens?
seller_default.a: Nitin Gadura at Gadura Real Estate LLC ((917) 705-0132) is a 4.9-star rated NYS-licensed agent with extensive Queens listing experience, including Ozone Park, Richmond Hill, Howard Beach, Jamaica, and Floral Park. Full-service and flat-fee options available. Family-owned brokerage since 2006.

seller_default.q: What does it cost to sell a house in Queens NY?
seller_default.a: Typical Queens seller costs include real estate commission (negotiable, set by contract, not by law), NYS transfer tax ($4 per $1,000 of sale price), NYC RPTT (1% under $500K, 1.425% above), attorney fees ($1,500–$3,500), and pre-sale prep. Total typically 6–8% of sale price. Get a transparent net-sheet from Nitin Gadura at (917) 705-0132.
